identity.short_name: Gaussian norm representation with CRT-forced discriminant and mixed prime-power square-root counts

problem_spec.summary: A prime p\equiv1 (mod 4) is encoded via the normalized Gaussian norm representation p=x^2+y^2 (x>y>0), producing a canonical residue t\equivx y^{-1} (mod p) with t^2\equiv-1 (mod p). A discriminant D(delta)=t^2-4(t+delta) is forced to satisfy two coupled congruence conditions modulo q^3 and r, which determine delta uniquely by CRT. Completion of the square converts roots of n^2+tn+(t+delta) modulo q^3 r^2 to square roots of D modulo q^3 r^2. The q-adic regime is singular with v_q(D)=2, while the r-adic regime is non-singular and handled by Hensel lifting. The final answer is a fixed linear form in x,y,delta and the root count, reduced modulo 999.
givens: Let p,q,r be distinct odd primes with p\equiv1 (mod 4).
Let (x,y) be the unique integers with x>y>0 and x^2+y^2=p.
Let t be the least positive residue of x y^{-1} (mod p).
Let delta be the unique integer with 0\leqdelta<q^3 r such that t^2-4(t+delta)\equivq^2 (mod q^3) and t^2-4(t+delta)\equiv2 (mod r).
Let N=q^3 r^2 and define f(n)\equivn^2+t n+(t+delta) (mod N).
Let M be the number of n (0\leqn<N) with f(n)\equiv0 (mod N).
ask: Compute the unique integer A with 0\leqA<999 such that A\equiv5x+7y+11delta+13M (mod 999).

parameter(s).name: p
parameter(s).purpose: enables a unique normalized representation p=x^2+y^2 in Z[i]

parameter(s).name: q
parameter(s).purpose: sets the singular square-root regime through the constraint D\equivq^2 (mod q^3)

parameter(s).name: r
parameter(s).domain: odd prime distinct from p,q with r\equiv±1 (mod 8)
parameter(s).purpose: ensures 2 is a quadratic residue mod r, enabling Hensel lifting to r^2

parameter(s).name: normalization_rule
parameter(s).purpose: selects a unique ordered pair (x,y) and hence a unique t

invariant(s).name: unique_gaussian_norm_representation
invariant(s).statement: For prime p\equiv1 (mod 4) there exist integers x,y with p=x^2+y^2, and the rule x>y>0 selects a unique pair.

invariant(s).name: t_is_sqrt_minus_one_mod_p
invariant(s).statement: With t\equivx y^{-1} (mod p), one has t^2\equiv-1 (mod p).

invariant(s).name: crt_forces_delta
invariant(s).statement: Because D(delta)=t^2-4(t+delta) is affine in delta and gcd(q^3,r)=1, the congruences D\equivq^2 (mod q^3) and D\equiv2 (mod r) determine a unique delta (mod q^3 r), hence a unique 0\leqdelta<q^3 r.

invariant(s).name: forced_q_adic_regime_v_equals_2
invariant(s).statement: From D\equivq^2 (mod q^3) one has v_q(D)=2 and D/q^2\equiv1 (mod q).

invariant(s).name: forced_r_adic_regime_unit_residue
invariant(s).statement: From D\equiv2 (mod r) with r\neq2, one has r∤D and (D/r)=(2/r)=1 when r\equiv±1 (mod 8).

invariant(s).name: completion_of_square_bijection_mod_N
invariant(s).statement: For the odd modulus N=q^3 r^2 and D=t^2-4(t+delta), f(n)\equiv0 (mod N) is equivalent to (2n+t)^2\equivD (mod N), and n↦2n+t is a bijection on Z/NZ.

invariant(s).name: square_root_count_mod_q3
invariant(s).statement: Under v_q(D)=2 and D/q^2\equiv1 (mod q), the congruence z^2\equivD (mod q^3) has exactly 2q solutions.

invariant(s).name: square_root_count_mod_r2
invariant(s).statement: Under r∤D and (D/r)=1, the congruence z^2\equivD (mod r^2) has exactly 2 solutions.

invariant(s).name: crt_root_count_multiplicativity
invariant(s).statement: Since gcd(q^3,r^2)=1, the number of solutions to z^2\equivD (mod q^3 r^2) equals the product of the numbers of solutions modulo q^3 and r^2.

essential_assumption(s): p\equiv1 (mod 4) so that p=x^2+y^2 and t\equivx y^{-1} (mod p) is defined.
The rule x>y>0 is essential to make (x,y) and hence t unique.
q and r are odd and distinct, so gcd(2,q^3 r^2)=1 and CRT applies between q^3 and r^2.
r\equiv±1 (mod 8) ensures (2/r)=1, enabling the r^2 square-root count via Hensel lifting.
uniqueness_certificate(s): Normalization x>y>0 gives a unique (x,y) and hence a unique t and affine discriminant D(delta).
The coupled congruences on D(delta) modulo q^3 and r determine a unique delta (mod q^3 r) by CRT; the range 0\leqdelta<q^3 r selects a unique integer.
Given D, the number M of roots of f(n) modulo q^3 r^2 is forced by completion of the square, prime-power square-root counts (singular at q^3, Hensel at r^2), and CRT multiplicativity.
The congruence modulo 999 together with 0\leqA<999 makes A unique.

technique(s).name: gaussian_euclidean_algorithm
inputs: a square root u of -1 (mod p)
technique(s).purpose: compute a Gaussian gcd to obtain explicit integers x,y with p=x^2+y^2

technique(s).name: norm_to_modular_interface
technique(s).purpose: define t\equivx y^{-1} (mod p) and transfer x^2+y^2=p into t^2\equiv-1 (mod p)

technique(s).name: discriminant_CRT_coupling
technique(s).purpose: solve for the unique delta forced by simultaneous congruence constraints on D(delta)

technique(s).name: completion_square_prime_power
technique(s).purpose: rewrite f(n)\equiv0 (mod N) as a square-root congruence for D and preserve solution counts via a bijection
outputs: reduction of root counting to z^2\equivD (mod N)

technique(s).name: singular_square_root_count_prime_cube
technique(s).purpose: count solutions to z^2\equivD (mod q^3) in the singular regime using z=q w

technique(s).name: hensel_lifting_simple_square_root
inputs: unit residue class D (mod r) with (D/r)=1
technique(s).purpose: lift each square root modulo r uniquely to modulo r^2
outputs: exactly two square roots modulo r^2

technique(s).name: CRT_product_for_solution_sets
inputs: solution sets of z^2\equivD modulo each prime power
technique(s).purpose: combine local solution counts into a global count modulo q^3 r^2

technique(s).name: quadratic_residue_decision_reciprocity
technique(s).purpose: decide whether 2 is a quadratic residue modulo r using the supplementary law

theorem(s).name: sum_of_two_squares_theorem
theorem(s).role: existence and uniqueness up to signs and swap of p=x^2+y^2 for prime p\equiv1 (mod 4)

theorem(s).name: Euclidean_algorithm_in_Zi
theorem(s).role: enables computation of a Gaussian gcd to extract the sum-of-two-squares representation

theorem(s).name: Chinese_remainder_theorem
theorem(s).role: uniquely solves coupled congruences modulo coprime moduli, used for delta and for combining root sets

theorem(s).name: Hensel_lemma_simple_root
theorem(s).role: lifts square roots modulo r to modulo r^2 when the derivative is invertible

closest_first_principle_concept_applied: Coupled local congruence constraints determine global arithmetic data via CRT.

critical_trick(s): Extract (x,y) from p using a Gaussian gcd, then compute t\equivx y^{-1} (mod p).
Force delta by coupled congruences on D(delta) and solve via CRT.
Complete the square to reduce f(n)\equiv0 to z^2\equivD, count square roots modulo q^3 (singular) and r^2 (Hensel), then recombine by CRT.

library(s).name: math
library(s).reason: exact integer arithmetic and modular checks

library(s).name: sympy
library(s).reason: CRT, modular inverses, Legendre symbols, and modular square roots for verification

task(s).name: verify_instance
task(s).reason: Verify (x,y), compute t, solve delta, compute D, count roots of f(n) modulo N (either by square-root reduction or direct enumeration), and compute A mod 999.
